reason: The item cleanly tests admissible inference boundaries from a simulation–experiment mismatch and a stated claim about parameter dominance, with correct options confined to “consistent with” and “not directly transferable” conclusions. Decoys are largely single-fault and plausible (bias attribution, parameter-importance inversion, SOC speculation, mechanism substitution), creating meaningful separation while avoiding dependence on obscure facts. Minor softness remains because the stem’s multi-parameter adjustment could invite over-interpretation about causality and relative influence if readers conflate “needed for fit” with “dominant driver.”
